edges.description: Visceral sphingomyelin storage and foamy macrophage infiltration produce hepatomegaly in the historical type E phenotype.
edges.source: Lysosomal Sphingomyelin and Lipid Storage with Foam Cell Infiltration

edges.description: Historical type E cases with lipid storage also showed chronic neurological deterioration, represented here as neurodegeneration.
edges.source: Lysosomal Sphingomyelin and Lipid Storage with Foam Cell Infiltration

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: These patients presented with hepatomegaly, foamy macrophage infiltration into tissues and bone marrow

nodes.description: Patients historically labeled type E showed partial acid sphingomyelinase deficiency with sphingomyelin accumulation and foamy macrophage (foam cell) infiltration of tissues and bone marrow, within the broader Niemann-Pick storage spectrum. The precise biochemical and molecular basis of the "type E" label was never clarified and is now subsumed under ASMD or NPC.
nodes.id: Lysosomal Sphingomyelin and Lipid Storage with Foam Cell Infiltration

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: These patients presented with hepatomegaly, foamy macrophage infiltration into tissues and bone marrow, partial deficiency of ASM activity, and moderate accumulation of sphingomyelin, but also exhibited chronic and often severe neurological deterioration.

nodes.description: Chronic, often severe neurological deterioration was reported.

evidence.reference_title: Types A and B Niemann-Pick disease.
evidence.snippet: but also exhibited chronic and often severe neurological deterioration
